pages.full_text:
__September/October.__ We have taken Margaret + helen out for 3 teas/lunches in the van.

__October 16th Friday.__
1.50. Simon, Eve & Tonic left for a w/end holiday. Bright sunshine on the M5 down past Bristol. We arrived at 5.40. at __Halse Farm campsite__ * * * * (middle of Exmoor) It's nice not having to wait until 4.10. to leave, when the children finish school. This is a working farm - cows, sheep, chicken + 2 barking sheep dogs. The farmer has boarded over the tractor wheels flush with the tyre, to keep mud out.

__Saturday__
A cold frost in the night. I had to get up twice to put more clothes on. We went on a 4 3/4 mile walk along Winsford Hill, down to Winsford village. Friendly P.O. good lunch. Walk back up the hill - one of the nicest I've been on, along a stream & up a very, very old track. No steep climbing. Tea. Now we're sitting in the sun with the van door open.
photo_captions: Looking up the Punch Bowl, Winsford Hill. We walked around this.
pages.notes: No handwritten page number visible at top-left. The text at the very top is a continuation from the previous spread (mid-sentence). The sideways caption 'Looking up the Punch Bowl, Winsford Hill. We walked around this.' runs along the right margin and continues as a photo caption on the right page — transcribed here as the caption for the photo visible on the right page. 'October 16th Friday' falls on a Friday in 2009 and 2015 within the volume B range (2003–2019); year cannot be confirmed from page content alone. '1.50' likely means 1:50pm departure time. 'w/end' = weekend. 'P.O.' = Post Office. 'Tonic' appears to be a pet (dog, mentioned in nearby spreads).

Winsford Village - we walked through this, lots of bridges.

The Royal Oak Inn, Winsford where we had lunch - the peanut butter ice cream

The top-left photo caption ('Looking up the Punch Bowl, Winsford Hill. We walked around this.') also appears as sideways marginalia on the left page — transcribed on both pages as it belongs to the right page photo.
